city_report.transport: Sofia Metro Line 4 (Yellow) operates service to the airport between 05:30 and 24:00. The stop is a few metres outside of Terminal 2; follow signs for "Subway" until you leave the southeast corner of the building. The journey to the city centre takes 20–30 minutes.

attraction.name: Nevsky Cathedral
attraction.description: One of the largest Orthodox cathedrals in the world.

attraction.name: Vitosha Boulevard
attraction.description: Main commercial street with views of Vitosha Mountain.

attraction.name: Serdica Ruins
attraction.description: Ancient Roman ruins in the center of Sofia.

attraction.name: St. George Rotunda
attraction.description: The oldest building in Sofia, a 4th-century church.

attraction.name: Banya Bashi Mosque
attraction.description: 16th-century mosque built over natural thermal spas.

attraction.name: National History Museum
attraction.description: Largest museum in Bulgaria with the Thracian treasures.

attraction.name: Sveta Nedelya
attraction.description: Medieval Orthodox cathedral in the heart of the city.

city_report.description: Situata ai piedi del monte Vitosha, Sofia offre costi di vita imbattibili e un accesso immediato alla natura montana. È una delle capitali emergenti con il più alto potenziale di crescita economica e urbana.
city_report.wiki_intro: Sofia (София) is the capital and largest city of Bulgaria, with about 2 million citizens (including suburbs). It is a dynamic European capital, distinguished by its unique combination of European and communist-style architecture as well as many beautiful Orthodox churches, including the grand Alexander Nevsky Cathedral and the medieval frescos of Boyana Church (a UNESCO World Heritage Site)....
